faqs.question: What are the most common pantry pests in Irvington homes?
faqs.answer: Irvington residents most frequently encounter Indian meal moths, flour beetles, rice weevils, and cigarette beetles. These pests infest dry goods like flour, cereal, pasta, rice, nuts, and pet food. Our technicians conduct thorough inspections to identify the specific pest species and all infested products in your pantry.

faqs.question: How quickly can you provide pantry pest control service in Irvington?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service throughout Irvington, New Jersey. Most pantry pest treatments take 1-2 hours depending on the extent of infestation and the size of your kitchen and storage areas. We'll schedule a convenient appointment time that works with your busy schedule.

faqs.question: What does the $350-$550 pantry pest control service include?
faqs.answer: Our service includes a complete inspection of all food storage areas, identification of infested products, targeted treatment of cracks and crevices where pests hide, application of safe residual products, pheromone monitoring traps, and detailed prevention recommendations. The price varies based on property size and infestation severity, with most Irvington homes falling in the mid-range.

faqs.question: How can I prevent pantry pests from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Store all dry goods in airtight containers, regularly clean shelves and corners, inspect groceries before storing them, rotate older products to the front, and dispose of infested items immediately. We provide customized prevention tips during service and offer follow-up visits to ensure your Irvington home remains pest-free long-term.
